summary: Wireformat capture attempted for deepseek + huggingface. DeepSeek: fetch interceptor installed but buf=0 — DeepSeek likely uses a non-fetch streaming method (EventSource or custom XHR). API endpoint confirmed as /api/v0/chat/completion via performance.getEntriesByType. Clean prompts ('Write a hello world function in Python', 'What is 2+2?') both rendered successfully. HuggingFace: not attempted yet (context budget exceeded). Recommend HAR capture via DevTools Export as alternative approach.

deepseek.notes: Fetch interceptor (window.fetch override) installed successfully (DS_INTERCEPTOR_READY title confirmed). Two clean prompts sent and responded to normally. However window.__ds.buf remained empty (length=0). Root cause: DeepSeek does NOT use window.fetch for SSE streaming — it likely uses EventSource API or a custom XMLHttpRequest-based streamer that bypasses window.fetch entirely. The performance.getEntriesByType('resource') confirms the URL is /api/v0/chat/completion. Alternative approach needed: either (1) override EventSource constructor, (2) override XMLHttpRequest.prototype.open/send, (3) use DevTools HAR export, or (4) use Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) via remote debugging.
deepseek.fetch_interceptor_result: INSTALLED_BUT_EMPTY — fetch not used for SSE

huggingface.notes: NOT ATTEMPTED in this cycle — context budget exceeded after DeepSeek debugging. From #454 probe, HuggingFace uses window.fetch for NDJSON streaming (interceptor worked in #454 and captured full stream). The same fetch interceptor approach should work for HuggingFace. Recommend re-running with HuggingFace-specific interceptor in next request.

findings.deepseek_streaming_method: NOT window.fetch. Likely EventSource or custom XHR. This explains why APF envelope injection via fetch override doesn't work for DeepSeek — the streaming happens through a different API.
findings.huggingface_streaming_method: window.fetch confirmed (from #454). Interceptor captured full NDJSON stream successfully in earlier probe.
findings.recommendation: For DeepSeek: try EventSource override or HAR export. For HuggingFace: re-run fetch interceptor (known to work from #454). Alternative: use Chrome --remote-debugging-port for CDP-based network capture.

next_steps: Re-attempt with EventSource override for DeepSeek
Re-attempt with fetch interceptor for HuggingFace (known working from #454)
Consider HAR export via DevTools as simpler alternative for both
